behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser + downstream hook-input handling handoff, not just a pure parse call. `MOCK-MISS`: a unit test of only `JSON.parse` would miss wiring/field-mapping regressions after parse. `REAL-INFRA`: behavior text does not require real process/OS/network. `LLM-DEP` and `MULTI-STEP`: none. Rejected `System` because behavior says normal hook input handling, not real process execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run in-process hook input pipeline modules together (payload ingestion function -> parse wrapper -> hook input normalizer/validator) with valid JSON fixtures that include code fences, backticks, and `$vars` inside string fields; assert parse success and expected normalized object reaches normal handling path with no parse-failure trace.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: This behavior validates interaction between parse failure detection and trace-record construction/content. `MOCK-MISS`: unit-testing only parser exception text misses whether trace payload includes diagnostic context. `REAL-INFRA`: no explicit need for real process or OS primitives in this behavior text. `LLM-DEP`/`MULTI-STEP`: none. Rejected `System` because required outcome is trace record content, not process/IO semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Use malformed JSON fixtures (without artificial trailing-junk-only bias) through in-process parse-and-trace path; assert emitted trace event type is `json_parse_failed` and includes location/context fields (e.g., offset/snippet/key path) sufficient to identify malformed region.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about mapping parser error state into policy result + telemetry intent, i.e., module handoff. `MOCK-MISS`: pure unit of one mapper may miss contract mismatch between parser-error shape and policy consumer. `REAL-INFRA`: explicitly says local path, so no real OS/network/subprocess required. `LLM-DEP`/`MULTI-STEP`: none. Rejected `System` by concrete text `local error-policy path`.
behaviors.test_description: Execute parser wrapper + error-policy module together in-process with malformed JSON input; assert returned result is handled (no exception escapes) and telemetry-intent flag/enum is set to parse-failure classification.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real subprocess/stdin/exit behavior. `REAL-INFRA` is yes: crash/no-crash and pre-exit signal ordering can differ in real process execution. Mocking process boundaries can hide failures (`MOCK-HIDE`). `LLM-DEP`/`MULTI-STEP`: none. Rejected `Agentic`/`Workflow` because no AI-model decision is part of correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the actual hook executable as a subprocess, write malformed bytes/JSON to stdin, close stdin, wait for exit; assert process exits without crash, and expected `json_parse_failed` trace signal is emitted before termination (via captured stderr/stdout or configured sink artifact timestamp/order).

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: End-to-end invocation path includes real stdin ingestion and real trace sink emission artifact; failure can occur at OS/process/IO boundaries. In-process fakes may miss buffering/flushing/path issues (`MOCK-HIDE`). No AI dependency; no multi-agent sequence. Rejected `Agentic`/`Workflow` because behavior contains no model-driven decision point.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke hook through its real entrypoint with malformed stdin payload and configured real trace sink (file/stdout channel used in production path); assert expected artifact/event exists and matches parse-failure signal schema.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: Requires comparative behavior across two input classes through same parse/error-attribution pipeline, typically parser + trace classification modules. Unit-only parser tests can miss attribution/tagging regressions across modules (`MOCK-MISS`). `REAL-INFRA`: no explicit process/OS requirement in behavior text. `LLM-DEP`/`MULTI-STEP`: none. Rejected `System` because distinction is logical attribution, not real subprocess semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run paired in-process fixtures through shared hook input pipeline: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings, (b) truly malformed JSON. Assert only (b) emits parse-failure classification/trace, while (a) stays on normal handling path; enforce this as regression matrix to prevent markdown false positives.
